seo.title: Django REST Framework vs Celery vs PostgreSQL
seo.description: A practical comparison of Django REST Framework, Celery, and PostgreSQL for Django.

content.intro: Comparing Django REST Framework and FastAPI for API development in Python ecosystems, focusing on implementation effort, scalability, and integration with Django's ecosystem.

options.name: Django REST Framework
options.tagline: Batteries-included API layer for Django projects
options.best_for: Existing Django applications requiring rapid API development with ORM integration

options.name: FastAPI
options.tagline: Modern API framework for high-performance applications
options.best_for: New projects prioritizing async support, type hints, and scalability

criteria.name: Ease of Integration with Django
criteria.description: Seamlessness with existing Django projects and ORM

values.Django REST Framework: Seamless integration with Django's ORM and existing codebase
values.FastAPI: Requires additional setup but works well with Django via middleware or separate services

values.Django REST Framework: Limited support - requires ASGI and async views with Django 3.1+
values.FastAPI: Built-in async support with Python's async/await syntax

criteria.name: Performance
criteria.description: Request handling speed and resource efficiency

values.Django REST Framework: Moderate performance - optimized for simplicity over raw speed
values.FastAPI: High performance - comparable to Node.js and Go in benchmarks

values.Django REST Framework: Low - follows Django's familiar patterns and conventions
values.FastAPI: Moderate - requires understanding of async programming and type hints

values.Django REST Framework: Extensive documentation and large user base
values.FastAPI: Growing community with active development and modern tooling

criteria.name: Scalability
criteria.description: Ability to handle increasing traffic and complexity

values.FastAPI: Designed for horizontal scaling and microservices architecture

criteria.name: Type Hints
criteria.description: Built-in support for static type checking

values.FastAPI: Full type hinting integration with Pydantic models
Automatic OpenAPI and Swagger UI documentation

verdict.summary: Django REST Framework offers deeper integration with existing Django projects but lacks native async support. FastAPI provides better performance and modern features but requires architectural adjustments for Django legacy systems.

use_cases.scenario: Existing Django application needing API endpoints
use_cases.reason: Leverages Django's ORM and middleware without architectural changes
Built-in async support and better scalability for modern architectures

use_cases.scenario: Team familiar with Django's patterns but needing async capabilities
use_cases.reason: Use FastAPI for async components and Django REST Framework for legacy integration
